headline: Kelantan's RM3.1 billion federal allocation tracking remains opaque
context: Kelantan received RM3.1 billion in federal development allocations in 2025 but the state government published no breakdown of how funds were distributed across projects.

cards.big: RM3.1 billion sent to Kelantan — state won't show where it went
cards.sub: Federal money enters state system and disappears from public tracking

cards.big: Federal-state fiscal transfer has no mandatory project-level reporting requirement
cards.sub: States receive block grants with wide discretion and minimal accountability

cards.big: Kelantan ranks last in infrastructure development index despite receiving RM3.1 billion
cards.sub: Billions in allocation have not translated into measurable development outcomes

cards.big: PAS-led government has controlled Kelantan since 1990 — 36 consecutive years
cards.sub: Long tenure without alternation creates deep entrenchment of spending patterns

cards.big: Federal allocation without state-level tracking is giving money without expecting receipts
Should federal development funds require project-level tracking regardless of state politics?
